questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Describe melting using the particle model
questions.question: What happens during melting?
options: The particles become colder as soon as the heater is turned on.
The mass of the substance decreases because particles disappear.
The particles stop moving completely until all the solid has melted.
Energy is used to weaken bonds between particles, so temperature stays constant while the substance melts.
questions.correctAnswer: Energy is used to weaken bonds between particles, so temperature stays constant while the substance melts.
questions.explanation: During melting, energy goes into overcoming forces between particles rather than increasing temperature.
questions.senNote: During melting, energy goes into overcoming forces between particles rather than increasing temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain the effect of reducing volume on gas pressure
questions.question: A gas is trapped in a syringe and compressed. What happens to the pressure?
options: The pressure falls because particles get smaller.
The pressure stays constant because mass does not change.
The pressure becomes zero because the gas cannot move.
The pressure increases because the same number of particles collide with the walls in a smaller volume.
questions.correctAnswer: The pressure increases because the same number of particles collide with the walls in a smaller volume.
questions.explanation: With less space, particles hit the container walls more often, so pressure rises.
questions.senNote: With less space, particles hit the container walls more often, so pressure rises.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain why gas pressure changes with temperature
questions.question: What happens to the gas pressure in a sealed container when the temperature rises?
options: The pressure falls because the particles lose mass.
The pressure stays the same because the number of particles does not change.
The pressure becomes zero because the particles spread out.
The pressure increases because the particles move faster and hit the walls more often.
questions.correctAnswer: The pressure increases because the particles move faster and hit the walls more often.
questions.explanation: Hotter particles move faster, so collisions with the container walls are more frequent and harder.
questions.senNote: Hotter particles move faster, so collisions with the container walls are more frequent and harder.

The pressure stays the same because the number of particles does not change.
The pressure becomes zero because the particles spread out.
The pressure increases because the particles move faster and hit the walls more often.
questions.correctAnswer: The pressure increases because the particles move faster and hit the walls more often.
questions.explanation: Hotter particles move faster, so collisions with the container walls are more frequent and harder.
questions.senNote: Hotter particles move faster, so collisions with the container walls are more frequent and harder.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Interpret a heating curve
questions.question: On a heating curve, what happens while the substance is boiling?
options: The temperature rises quickly because all the energy increases particle speed.
The mass doubles because bubbles are formed.
The particles stop moving while the liquid changes into gas.
The temperature stays constant while energy is transferred to separate the particles.
questions.correctAnswer: The temperature stays constant while energy is transferred to separate the particles.
questions.explanation: During boiling, energy is used to overcome forces between particles rather than increase temperature.
questions.senNote: During boiling, energy is used to overcome forces between particles rather than increase temperature.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.skill: Explain Brownian motion
questions.question: Why does Brownian motion happen?
options: The particles are pulled by magnetic fields in the air.
The particles are heavy so they stop moving quickly.
The particles only move when light shines on them.
Small visible particles are hit unevenly by moving molecules.
questions.correctAnswer: Small visible particles are hit unevenly by moving molecules.
questions.explanation: Brownian motion is caused by random collisions from moving molecules.
questions.senNote: Brownian motion is caused by random collisions from moving molecules.
